_meta.title: Hydrogen non-relativistic energy eigenvalues E_n
_meta.formula: E_n = -1 / (2 n^2)  Hartree  (Schroedinger, infinite nuclear mass)

source.name: NIST Atomic Spectra Database (Levels)
source.notes: Values quoted to the level of precision the non-relativistic Schroedinger formula achieves; experimental NIST values include relativistic + QED + finite-mass corrections that shift each level by < 1.4 x 10^-5 Hartree (Lamb shift dominant), so the comparison tolerance below is set well above that ceiling.

tolerance.rationale: We test the closed-form implementation E_n = -0.5/n^2, not the experimental value. Tolerance is double-precision round-off, not physical accuracy — i.e. this verifies the implementation reproduces the formula, with the formula itself documented above.
